This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. 2026-04-17T07:16:52.0309014Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:16:32.692172094Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":38185,\"title\":\"[Bug] `@azure/communication-calling` bundles vulnerable `axios@1.8.4` (multiple unpatched CVEs)\",\"body\":\"\\n## Package Name\\n`@azure/communication-calling`\\n\\n## Package Version\\n- Tested: `1.38.1`, `1.42.1` (latest stable as of 2026-04-17)\\n- All versions from at least `1.38.1` through `1.42.1` ship the same bundled `axios@1.8.4`.\\n- Also verified `1.43.1-beta.1` (next tag) — same embedded `axios@1.8.4`.\\n\\n## Operating System\\nAny (issue is in the published npm package, independent of OS).\\n\\n## Browser / Runtime\\nAny (issue is in the pre-built bundle).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Describe the bug\\n\\n`@azure/communication-calling` distributes a single pre-built bundle (`dist/sdk.bundle.js` and `dist-esm/sdk.bundle.js`) that **embeds `axios@1.8.4`** directly inside the bundle. Because axios is not a declared dependency and is not resolved at install time, **downstream applications have no way to upgrade or override this version** via `package.json`, `overrides`, `resolutions`, or webpack configuration.\\n\\n`axios@1.8.4` has multiple publicly disclosed, unpatched CVEs. This surfaces in every consumer\\u0026#39;s vulnerability scan and cannot be remediated on our side.\\n\\n### Affected CVEs still present in axios 1.8.4\\n\\n| CVE | Type | Fixed in axios |\\n|-----|------|----------------|\\n| CVE-2026-25639 | Denial of Service via `__proto__` key in `mergeConfig` (CVSS 7.5 High) | 1.13.5 |\\n| CVE-2025-58754 | Denial of Service via unbounded `data:` URI decoding (Node.js; CVSS 7.5) | 1.12.0 |\\n| CVE-2026-39865 | Denial of Service via HTTP/2 session cleanup (CVSS 8.2 High) | 1.13.2 |\\n\\nReferences:\\n- https://github.com/axios/axios/security/advisories/GHSA-43fc-jf86-j433\\n- https://security.snyk.io/package/npm/axios/1.8.4\\n\\n---\\n\\n## To Reproduce\\n\\n```bash\\n# Install latest stable\\nnpm install @azure/communication-calling@1.42.1\\n\\n# 1) Confirm axios is NOT a declared dependency\\ncat node_modules/@azure/communication-calling/package.json | grep -i axios\\n# → only an `overrides` entry for axios-\\u0026gt;follow-redirects; axios itself is bundled\\n\\n# 2) Confirm axios 1.8.4 is embedded inside the pre-built bundle\\ngrep -c \\u0026#39;1\\\\.8\\\\.4\\u0026#39; node_modules/@azure/communication-calling/dist/sdk.bundle.js\\n# → 1   (the axios VERSION constant)\\n\\ngrep -o \\u0026#39;formatMessage.\\\\{0,80\\\\}Axios v.\\\\{0,40\\\\}\\u0026#39; node_modules/@azure/communication-calling/dist/sdk.bundle.js\\n# → ...return\\u0026#34;[Axios v\\u0026#34;+st+\\u0026#34;] Transitional option...\\n#   with  const st=\\u0026#34;1.8.4\\u0026#34;  assigned nearby\\n\\n# 3) Same result for dist-esm\\ngrep -c \\u0026#39;1\\\\.8\\\\.4\\u0026#39; node_modules/@azure/communication-calling/dist-esm/sdk.bundle.js\\n# → 1\\n```\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe SDK bundle should not embed a version of axios that contains publicly known, unpatched CVEs. Either:\\n\\n1. **Bump the bundled axios to `1.15.0+`** (which closes all three CVEs above and is also past the 2026-03-31 supply-chain compromised versions `1.14.1` / `0.30.4`), OR\\n2. **Declare `axios` as a regular `dependencies` entry** rather than bundling it, so that consumers can apply `overrides` / `resolutions`, OR\\n3. **Document an officially recognized \\u0026#34;not exploitable\\u0026#34; stance** (with CVSS Environmental-metric reasoning) that auditors and SCA vendors (Snyk, Trivy, Mend, Black Duck, GitHub Advanced Security, etc.) can rely on.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\n- `axios@1.8.4` string identity (`\\u0026#34;1.8.4\\u0026#34;`, `[Axios v...]`) and behavioral fingerprint are embedded verbatim.\\n- SCA tools flag every downstream project.\\n- Consumers cannot upgrade; `@azure/communication-calling/package.json` only declares `@azure/communication-common` and `@azure/logger` as runtime deps. There is no axios entry to override.\\n- The embedded `overrides` block `{\\u0026#34;axios\\u0026#34;:{\\u0026#34;follow-redirects\\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34;1.15.6\\u0026#34;}}` applies only at install-time, and since axios itself is bundled, it has no effect on the `sdk.bundle.js` that is actually executed.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nEvery project that depends on `@azure/communication-calling` inherits three unresolvable High-severity CVE findings. Specific downstream pain points:\\n\\n- **Compliance / audit**: SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI-DSS and similar programs require remediation or a documented risk acceptance per CVE; the current state forces every customer to independently author the same acceptance rationale.\\n- **CI pipelines**: `npm audit`, Dependabot, Snyk, GitHub Advanced Security, Trivy etc. will break builds that are configured to fail on High severity.\\n- **Enterprise procurement**: vendors performing third-party risk assessment on applications using ACS Calling will raise repeated queries that Microsoft alone can retire authoritatively.\\n\\nWhile in most configurations the embedded axios is not directly reachable from application code (the instance is scoped to SDK-internal calls to ACS endpoints, so CVE-2026-25639 prototype config, CVE-2025-58754 `data:` URIs, and CVE-2026-39865 HTTP/2 attacker-controlled server are not trivially triggerable), the burden of proving non-exploitability is currently pushed to every customer.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Suggested Remediation (in priority order)\\n\\n1. **Short term** – rebuild and re-publish the SDK with `axios@1.15.0` (or newer) bundled. No API surface change required.\\n2. **Medium term** – stop bundling axios. Declare it as a peer/runtime dependency so that customers can apply `overrides` when a new CVE is disclosed, exactly as was done for `@azure/communication-sms` / `xml2js` in #27169.\\n3. **Long term** – publish an official security posture statement for `@azure/communication-calling` that enumerates bundled third-party components and their CVE status, so SCA suppressions can cite a Microsoft-authored document.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Additional context\\n\\n- Related precedent: #27169 (`@azure/communication-sms` depends on a vulnerable `xml2js`) was fixed within one month by updating the transitive dependency.\\n- The newer beta `1.43.1-beta.1` (next tag) was also checked and exhibits the same embedded `axios@1.8.4`.\\n- The 2026-03-31 axios supply-chain incident (malicious `1.14.1` / `0.30.4`) reinforces the need to move to `1.15.0+`; any future bump should explicitly skip `1.14.x`.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/38185\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"yiming\",\"id\":333365,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/yiming\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/333365?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"labels\":[\"question\",\"customer-reported\",\"needs-triage\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T07:14:41Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T07:15:22Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-17T07:16:52.0333273Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:16:32.710909582Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":38185,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-js"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T07:16:52.0361149Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:16:32.907542592Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":38185,\"title\":\"[Bug] `@azure/communication-calling` bundles vulnerable `axios@1.8.4` (multiple unpatched CVEs)\",\"body\":\"\\n## Package Name\\n`@azure/communication-calling`\\n\\n## Package Version\\n- Tested: `1.38.1`, `1.42.1` (latest stable as of 2026-04-17)\\n- All versions from at least `1.38.1` through `1.42.1` ship the same bundled `axios@1.8.4`.\\n- Also verified `1.43.1-beta.1` (next tag) — same embedded `axios@1.8.4`.\\n\\n## Operating System\\nAny (issue is in the published npm package, independent of OS).\\n\\n## Browser / Runtime\\nAny (issue is in the pre-built bundle).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Describe the bug\\n\\n`@azure/communication-calling` distributes a single pre-built bundle (`dist/sdk.bundle.js` and `dist-esm/sdk.bundle.js`) that **embeds `axios@1.8.4`** directly inside the bundle. Because axios is not a declared dependency and is not resolved at install time, **downstream applications have no way to upgrade or override this version** via `package.json`, `overrides`, `resolutions`, or webpack configuration.\\n\\n`axios@1.8.4` has multiple publicly disclosed, unpatched CVEs. This surfaces in every consumer\\u0026#39;s vulnerability scan and cannot be remediated on our side.\\n\\n### Affected CVEs still present in axios 1.8.4\\n\\n| CVE | Type | Fixed in axios |\\n|-----|------|----------------|\\n| CVE-2026-25639 | Denial of Service via `__proto__` key in `mergeConfig` (CVSS 7.5 High) | 1.13.5 |\\n| CVE-2025-58754 | Denial of Service via unbounded `data:` URI decoding (Node.js; CVSS 7.5) | 1.12.0 |\\n| CVE-2026-39865 | Denial of Service via HTTP/2 session cleanup (CVSS 8.2 High) | 1.13.2 |\\n\\nReferences:\\n- https://github.com/axios/axios/security/advisories/GHSA-43fc-jf86-j433\\n- https://security.snyk.io/package/npm/axios/1.8.4\\n\\n---\\n\\n## To Reproduce\\n\\n```bash\\n# Install latest stable\\nnpm install @azure/communication-calling@1.42.1\\n\\n# 1) Confirm axios is NOT a declared dependency\\ncat node_modules/@azure/communication-calling/package.json | grep -i axios\\n# → only an `overrides` entry for axios-\\u0026gt;follow-redirects; axios itself is bundled\\n\\n# 2) Confirm axios 1.8.4 is embedded inside the pre-built bundle\\ngrep -c \\u0026#39;1\\\\.8\\\\.4\\u0026#39; node_modules/@azure/communication-calling/dist/sdk.bundle.js\\n# → 1   (the axios VERSION constant)\\n\\ngrep -o \\u0026#39;formatMessage.\\\\{0,80\\\\}Axios v.\\\\{0,40\\\\}\\u0026#39; node_modules/@azure/communication-calling/dist/sdk.bundle.js\\n# → ...return\\u0026#34;[Axios v\\u0026#34;+st+\\u0026#34;] Transitional option...\\n#   with  const st=\\u0026#34;1.8.4\\u0026#34;  assigned nearby\\n\\n# 3) Same result for dist-esm\\ngrep -c \\u0026#39;1\\\\.8\\\\.4\\u0026#39; node_modules/@azure/communication-calling/dist-esm/sdk.bundle.js\\n# → 1\\n```\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe SDK bundle should not embed a version of axios that contains publicly known, unpatched CVEs. Either:\\n\\n1. **Bump the bundled axios to `1.15.0+`** (which closes all three CVEs above and is also past the 2026-03-31 supply-chain compromised versions `1.14.1` / `0.30.4`), OR\\n2. **Declare `axios` as a regular `dependencies` entry** rather than bundling it, so that consumers can apply `overrides` / `resolutions`, OR\\n3. **Document an officially recognized \\u0026#34;not exploitable\\u0026#34; stance** (with CVSS Environmental-metric reasoning) that auditors and SCA vendors (Snyk, Trivy, Mend, Black Duck, GitHub Advanced Security, etc.) can rely on.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\n- `axios@1.8.4` string identity (`\\u0026#34;1.8.4\\u0026#34;`, `[Axios v...]`) and behavioral fingerprint are embedded verbatim.\\n- SCA tools flag every downstream project.\\n- Consumers cannot upgrade; `@azure/communication-calling/package.json` only declares `@azure/communication-common` and `@azure/logger` as runtime deps. There is no axios entry to override.\\n- The embedded `overrides` block `{\\u0026#34;axios\\u0026#34;:{\\u0026#34;follow-redirects\\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34;1.15.6\\u0026#34;}}` applies only at install-time, and since axios itself is bundled, it has no effect on the `sdk.bundle.js` that is actually executed.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nEvery project that depends on `@azure/communication-calling` inherits three unresolvable High-severity CVE findings. Specific downstream pain points:\\n\\n- **Compliance / audit**: SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI-DSS and similar programs require remediation or a documented risk acceptance per CVE; the current state forces every customer to independently author the same acceptance rationale.\\n- **CI pipelines**: `npm audit`, Dependabot, Snyk, GitHub Advanced Security, Trivy etc. will break builds that are configured to fail on High severity.\\n- **Enterprise procurement**: vendors performing third-party risk assessment on applications using ACS Calling will raise repeated queries that Microsoft alone can retire authoritatively.\\n\\nWhile in most configurations the embedded axios is not directly reachable from application code (the instance is scoped to SDK-internal calls to ACS endpoints, so CVE-2026-25639 prototype config, CVE-2025-58754 `data:` URIs, and CVE-2026-39865 HTTP/2 attacker-controlled server are not trivially triggerable), the burden of proving non-exploitability is currently pushed to every customer.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Suggested Remediation (in priority order)\\n\\n1. **Short term** – rebuild and re-publish the SDK with `axios@1.15.0` (or newer) bundled. No API surface change required.\\n2. **Medium term** – stop bundling axios.
